message:
fix(doctor): skip upstream warning when no git remotes exist

Cherry-picked from PR #1915 by @maphew. In a freshly initialized repo
with no remotes, CheckGitUpstream previously warned about missing
upstream tracking. Now returns OK when `git remote` reports nothing.

Co-Authored-By: Matt Wilkie <maphew@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
